This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} year ({{jalink|702 year|Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{jalink|Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214}}, who came from the {{jalink|Keno clan}} clan. Genealogy of the Madarame family name of Marquis Kichiya]” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2016. “Overview of the Keno clan” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2005. . In "{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}," it is listed as ``Shoshimine Shrine,'' and it is listed as a Meishin Taisha Shrine, and it received an official gift from the government (a dedication of a coin on top of the {{jalink|Shingu|Q11588759|draft}} from the Jingikan during festivals such as the Tsutsugi Festival and the Niiname Festival).In the Middle Ages, this area belonged to the {{jalink|Sendai domain|Q1062182}} and was revered by the {{jalink|Date family}}, who donated their shrine territory and reconstructed and repaired the shrine, but in {{jalink|An'ei}}8 ({{jalink|1779|Q7714}}), the shrine was destroyed by wildfire. In April of {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 8th year ({{jalink|1796|Q7818}}), the lord of Sendai rebuilt the shrine, and the shrine was completed in 1806. The shrine hall and worship hall were built in {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}7 ({{jalink|1918|Q2094}}), and the shrine building was renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}11 ({{jalink|1999|Q2091}}). Additionally, our company is considered to be the main guardian shrine of the former Uda District. In the old days, it was called ``Katsuzensha'' and ``Souzengu Shrine,'' and it is still called ``Oku no Sozengu Shrine.'' Sozen is a deity also known as ``Aozakigami,'' and is worshiped as the guardian deity of horses in regions ranging from the Tohoku region to the Kanto-Chubu region. === Seven wonders of Kobirei Shrine === There is something called the Seven Wonders within our company's grounds. *Cherry blossoms in the southern sky : It is located on the south side of the company grounds, and is called the ``Nanten cherry tree'' because the flowers bloom in one place like the Nanten fruit. *Hokuto no Matsu : It is located on the east side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and it is said that it curves toward the north because a princess who washed ashore on the shore clung to it during childbirth. *Nana-leaf oak : Located on the west side of the shrine grounds, it was named because its leaves grow in groups of seven. The original ``Nanaha no Kashiwa'' tree fell down in a rainstorm in September 1907, so the chief priest at the time planted an oak sapling in its place. *Kagamigaike : A pond with a circumference of about 3.7 meters located in a valley on the east side of the shrine grounds, with sacred water springing up nearby and flowing into the pond. It is said that this water and pond have never dried up even during any drought. Kagamigaike Pond is named after the legend that a princess used the water in the pond as a mirror. *One-leaf reed (Yoshi) : It is found around Kagamigaike Pond and is said to have leaves only on one side of the stem. It is said that the gods Yoshibetsu and Hakuhime fed horses with this reed as food. *bud white bamboo : This bamboo grows on the hills around Kagamigaike Pond and is called ``Mejiro no Sasa'' because its buds are white. It is said that if a horse eats regular bamboo, it will get sick, but if a horse eats sprouted bamboo, it will not get sick. It is also said that feeding horses with sprouted bamboo soaked in the sacred waters of Kagamigaike is effective against horse illnesses. *Satsuki of Kuyo : It is located on the west side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and is said to have nine flowers blooming in white. Of the Seven Wonders, it is said that the cherry blossoms in the southern sky, the seven-leaved oak, and the nine-leafed oak were brought as plants by a princess when she washed ashore. === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall of Nagarezukuri style. File:Honden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Main shrine
{{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}}. You can see the Date family crest, the vertical, three-hitter, and nine-day crest. File:Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Precincts of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Precincts There is a valley on the east side of the precincts, and if you go down the path on the slope, you will find a sacred spring and a pond called Kagami-ike Pond. Legend has it that the sacred water never dries up even in times of drought. There is also a shrine office and a parking lot within the precincts, and the hilly area to the south of the precincts is home to Hayama Park, complete with stairs and an observation deck. Inside Hayama Park, there is a place where the enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to have been buried, and a stone monument has been erected that reads, ``The burial place of Kobirei Shrine's enshrined deity.'' File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Sacred water and stone shrine File:Kagami-ike pond of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Kagami-ike pond and bamboo forestFile:Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine. File:View from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|View from Hayama Park to the east
The area around the {{jalink|Shinchi Power Plant|Q11501532|Shinchi Thermal Power Plant}} where smoke is rising is the Imakami area where the princess washed ashore. File:South view from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|South view from Hayama-kouen park File:2nd torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG| File:1st torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Setsusha === * Hayama Shrine (Hayama Shrine) :: The enshrined deity is Toyouke Hime no Mikoto, the same as Kobirei Shrine, and it is believed to be a shrine that protects safe childbirth and protects children from illness. Inside the shelter are pillows for praying for safe childbirth and a small shrine. In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.'' File:Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Hayama Shrine File:Inside of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Inside the cover
Enshrines a small shrine and a board sign File:Anzan-ishi(easy delivery stone) of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Easy delivery stone === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Soma District|Q1155850}}{{jalink|Shinchi Town|Q1205416}}{{jalink|Komagamine (Shinchi Town)|Q11668283|Komagamine}} Daisaku 44 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * “Kobei Shrine Visit Guide” (Kobei Shrine Office) * “Shinchi Town History” (Shinchi Town Board of Education 1993) === External link === * [https://kobiminejinja.jp/ Kobimine Shrine] (official website) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276004.html Kobirei Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kohimine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:新地町の建築物]] [[Category:新地町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名こひみねしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === * '''{{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}}''' : {{jalink|Toyouke Daijingu|Q11633343|Ise Jingu Outer Shrine}} is said to be the same deity, but there are various theories about the deity enshrined (see below). : Hisao Hoga is the founder of {{jalink|Kodanmine Shrine}} (Komayumi Shrine) in {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} and {{jalink|Komagata Shrine|Q11 in {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}. 668370}} (Komagata God) and claimed to be the same god "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm Overview of the Keno clan]” “Koju Kinoboma”, 2005. . It is said that Toyouke Hime no Mikoto has divine virtues such as good harvests, protection of safe childbirth, eradication of pestilence, and protection of sericulture.There is also a legend about the deity enshrined in our company and horses, so there is strong belief in horses, including protection of horses and prayers for recovery from horse illnesses. === About the enshrined deity === The current enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to be Toyouke Hime no Mikoto of the Geku of Ise Jingu, but the following are the main theories regarding the other enshrined deities. * Theory of {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}} : In {{jalink|Kunitsukami|Q60995523}} there was a god called Yoshibetsu no Kami. Yoshibetsu no Kami was a god with a long face, a large and strong body, and a ferocious heart. It is said that for this reason, the gods feared and followed the god of Yoshibetsu. However, many goddesses did not obey Yoshibetsunokami because she had an ugly appearance and a large private part, but only the goddess who was called ``Hakuhime no Kami'' because she was beautiful, had a large body, and had white hair on her body obeyed Yoshibetsunokami. Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' It is said that Yoshibetsunokami and Hakuhimekami kept horses in the Kozenmine area, and when the horses were sick, they treated them and taught the gods of the country how to raise horses. It is said that Kobirei Shrine was founded when the local people built a shrine and respectfully worshiped the two gods. * Separate story (old story): In the era of {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}}, a certain princess committed a sin (one theory is {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|Q3541665|It was because she could communicate with horses}}) that she carved an empty ship. It was carried ashore by a boat built by the Japanese government and washed ashore on the coast of Sugaya-go in {{jalink|Oshu|Q1175530}}{{jalink|Uda District|Q11449246}} (near the present-day Imagami district of Shinchi Town). The princess was pregnant, and with the help of a benevolent man named Gondayu Nukazuka who lived on the coast, he built a wooden hut for her to live in. Afterwards, they traveled along the mountain range and came to the area of ​​Zimei Ridge, where they built a wooden hut and made it their home. The princess gave birth to a child there, but the child's face resembled that of a horse. It is said that Gondayu Nukazuka worked with Hattayu Kanno, who was from the same village, to support the princess and her newborn child ({{jalink|Interracial Marriage Story|Q134875778}}). Kobirei Shrine is a shrine that enshrines a child with a face resembling a horse, and Hayama to the south of the shrine is a place where a princess is enshrined, and it is said that it is called ``Mother Mountain'' because her mother is enshrined there. : There is a similar theory in ``Hunai Fudoki'' that a mother, fearing the birth of a child with a horse's face, abandoned her child in a valley, and the {{jalink|monkey|Q1367|monkey}} who lived in that valley fed the child chewed leaves of the {{jalink|kuzu}} tree to raise it, and later enshrined the child as the deity of Kobirei. This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} year ({{jalink|702 year|Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{jalink|Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214}}, who came from the {{jalink|Keno clan}} clan. The area around the {{jalink|Shinchi Power Plant|Q11501532|Shinchi Thermal Power Plant}} where smoke is rising is the Imakami area where the princess washed ashore. File:South view from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|South view from Hayama-kouen park File:2nd torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG| File:1st torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Setsusha === * Hayama Shrine (Hayama Shrine) :: The enshrined deity is Toyouke Hime no Mikoto, the same as Kobirei Shrine, and it is believed to be a shrine that protects safe childbirth and protects children from illness. Inside the shelter are pillows for praying for safe childbirth and a small shrine. In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.'' File:Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Hayama Shrine File:Inside of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Inside the cover

{{ill|lt=July 7th (lunar calendar)|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=July 7th lunar calendar}} |Shinto ritual = Horse year Goennen Sengu Festival (once every 12 years)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto torii Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kobimine Shrine'' (Kobimine Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Soma District|Q1155850|WD=Q1155850}}{{ill|lt=Shinchi Town|Q1205416|WD=Q1205416}}. It is {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}}''' : {{ill|lt=Toyouke Daijingu|Q11633343|WD=Q11633343|lt=Ise Jingu Outer Shrine}} is said to be the same deity, but there are various theories about the deity enshrined (see below). : Hisao Hoga is the founder of {{ill||lt=Kodanmine Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Komayumi Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} and {{jalink|Komagata Shrine|Q11 in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}. 668370}} (Komagata God) and claimed to be the same god "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm Overview of the Keno clan]” “Koju Kinoboma”, 2005. . It is said that Toyouke Hime no Mikoto has divine virtues such as good harvests, protection of safe childbirth, eradication of pestilence, and protection of sericulture.There is also a legend about the deity enshrined in our company and horses, so there is strong belief in horses, including protection of horses and prayers for recovery from horse illnesses. === About the enshrined deity === The current enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to be Toyouke Hime no Mikoto of the Geku of Ise Jingu, but the following are the main theories regarding the other enshrined deities. * Theory of {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} : In {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}} there was a god called Yoshibetsu no Kami. Yoshibetsu no Kami was a god with a long face, a large and strong body, and a ferocious heart. It is said that for this reason, the gods feared and followed the god of Yoshibetsu. However, many goddesses did not obey Yoshibetsunokami because she had an ugly appearance and a large private part, but only the goddess who was called ``Hakuhime no Kami'' because she was beautiful, had a large body, and had white hair on her body obeyed Yoshibetsunokami. Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' It is said that Yoshibetsunokami and Hakuhimekami kept horses in the Kozenmine area, and when the horses were sick, they treated them and taught the gods of the country how to raise horses. It is said that Kobirei Shrine was founded when the local people built a shrine and respectfully worshiped the two gods. * Separate story (old story): In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}, a certain princess committed a sin (one theory is {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|lt=Q3541665}}) that she carved an empty ship. It was carried ashore by a boat built by the Japanese government and washed ashore on the coast of Sugaya-go in {{ill|lt=Oshu|Q1175530|WD=Q1175530}}{{ill|lt=Uda District|Q11449246|WD=Q11449246}} (near the present-day Imagami district of Shinchi Town). The princess was pregnant, and with the help of a benevolent man named Gondayu Nukazuka who lived on the coast, he built a wooden hut for her to live in. Afterwards, they traveled along the mountain range and came to the area of ​​Zimei Ridge, where they built a wooden hut and made it their home. The princess gave birth to a child there, but the child's face resembled that of a horse. It is said that Gondayu Nukazuka worked with Hattayu Kanno, who was from the same village, to support the princess and her newborn child ({{ill|lt=Interracial Marriage Story|Q134875778|WD=Q134875778}}). Kobirei Shrine is a shrine that enshrines a child with a face resembling a horse, and Hayama to the south of the shrine is a place where a princess is enshrined, and it is said that it is called ``Mother Mountain'' because her mother is enshrined there. : There is a similar theory in ``Hunai Fudoki'' that a mother, fearing the birth of a child with a horse's face, abandoned her child in a valley, and the {{ill|lt=monkey|Q1367|WD=Q1367|lt=monkey}} who lived in that valley fed the child chewed leaves of the {{ill||lt=kuzu|UNKNOWN}} tree to raise it, and later enshrined the child as the deity of Kobirei. This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} year ({{ill|lt=702 year|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{ill|lt=Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214|WD=Q108487214}}, who came from the {{ill||lt=Keno clan|UNKNOWN}} clan. Genealogy of the Madarame family name of Marquis Kichiya]” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2016. “Overview of the Keno clan” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2005. . In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," it is listed as ``Shoshimine Shrine,'' and it is listed as a Meishin Taisha Shrine, and it received an official gift from the government (a dedication of a coin on top of the {{ill|lt=Shingu|Q11588759|WD=Q11588759|lt=draft}} from the Jingikan during festivals such as the Tsutsugi Festival and the Niiname Festival).In the Middle Ages, this area belonged to the {{ill|lt=Sendai domain|Q1062182|WD=Q1062182}} and was revered by the {{ill||lt=Date family|UNKNOWN}}, who donated their shrine territory and reconstructed and repaired the shrine, but in {{ill||lt=An'ei|UNKNOWN}}8 ({{ill|lt=1779|Q7714|WD=Q7714}}), the shrine was destroyed by wildfire. In April of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|WD=Q7818}}), the lord of Sendai rebuilt the shrine, and the shrine was completed in 1806. The shrine hall and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}7 ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), and the shrine building was renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}11 ({{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091}}). Additionally, our company is considered to be the main guardian shrine of the former Uda District. In the old days, it was called ``Katsuzensha'' and ``Souzengu Shrine,'' and it is still called ``Oku no Sozengu Shrine.'' Sozen is a deity also known as ``Aozakigami,'' and is worshiped as the guardian deity of horses in regions ranging from the Tohoku region to the Kanto-Chubu region. === Seven wonders of Kobirei Shrine === There is something called the Seven Wonders within our company's grounds. *Cherry blossoms in the southern sky : It is located on the south side of the company grounds, and is called the ``Nanten cherry tree'' because the flowers bloom in one place like the Nanten fruit. *Hokuto no Matsu : It is located on the east side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and it is said that it curves toward the north because a princess who washed ashore on the shore clung to it during childbirth. *Nana-leaf oak : Located on the west side of the shrine grounds, it was named because its leaves grow in groups of seven. The original ``Nanaha no Kashiwa'' tree fell down in a rainstorm in September 1907, so the chief priest at the time planted an oak sapling in its place. *Kagamigaike : A pond with a circumference of about 3.7 meters located in a valley on the east side of the shrine grounds, with sacred water springing up nearby and flowing into the pond. It is said that this water and pond have never dried up even during any drought. Kagamigaike Pond is named after the legend that a princess used the water in the pond as a mirror. *One-leaf reed (Yoshi) : It is found around Kagamigaike Pond and is said to have leaves only on one side of the stem. It is said that the gods Yoshibetsu and Hakuhime fed horses with this reed as food. *bud white bamboo : This bamboo grows on the hills around Kagamigaike Pond and is called ``Mejiro no Sasa'' because its buds are white. It is said that if a horse eats regular bamboo, it will get sick, but if a horse eats sprouted bamboo, it will not get sick. It is also said that feeding horses with sprouted bamboo soaked in the sacred waters of Kagamigaike is effective against horse illnesses. *Satsuki of Kuyo : It is located on the west side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and is said to have nine flowers blooming in white. Of the Seven Wonders, it is said that the cherry blossoms in the southern sky, the seven-leaved oak, and the nine-leafed oak were brought as plants by a princess when she washed ashore. === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall of Nagarezukuri style. File:Honden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Main shrine
{{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. You can see the Date family crest, the vertical, three-hitter, and nine-day crest. File:Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Precincts of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Precincts There is a valley on the east side of the precincts, and if you go down the path on the slope, you will find a sacred spring and a pond called Kagami-ike Pond. Legend has it that the sacred water never dries up even in times of drought. There is also a shrine office and a parking lot within the precincts, and the hilly area to the south of the precincts is home to Hayama Park, complete with stairs and an observation deck. Inside Hayama Park, there is a place where the enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to have been buried, and a stone monument has been erected that reads, ``The burial place of Kobirei Shrine's enshrined deity.'' {{ill|lt=July 7th (lunar calendar)|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=July 7th lunar calendar}} |Shinto ritual = Horse year Goennen Sengu Festival (once every 12 years)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Shinto torii Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kobimine Shrine'' (Kobimine Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Soma District|Q1155850|WD=Q1155850}}{{ill|lt=Shinchi Town|Q1205416|WD=Q1205416}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}}''' : {{ill|lt=Toyouke Daijingu|Q11633343|WD=Q11633343|lt=Ise Jingu Outer Shrine}} is said to be the same deity, but there are various theories about the deity enshrined (see below). : Hisao Hoga is the founder of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kodanmine Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Komayumi Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} and {{jalink|Komagata Shrine|Q11 in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}. 668370}} (Komagata God) and claimed to be the same god "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm Overview of the Keno clan]” “Koju Kinoboma”, 2005. . It is said that Toyouke Hime no Mikoto has divine virtues such as good harvests, protection of safe childbirth, eradication of pestilence, and protection of sericulture.There is also a legend about the deity enshrined in our company and horses, so there is strong belief in horses, including protection of horses and prayers for recovery from horse illnesses. === About the enshrined deity === The current enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to be Toyouke Hime no Mikoto of the Geku of Ise Jingu, but the following are the main theories regarding the other enshrined deities. * Theory of {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} : In {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}} there was a god called Yoshibetsu no Kami. Yoshibetsu no Kami was a god with a long face, a large and strong body, and a ferocious heart. It is said that for this reason, the gods feared and followed the god of Yoshibetsu. However, many goddesses did not obey Yoshibetsunokami because she had an ugly appearance and a large private part, but only the goddess who was called ``Hakuhime no Kami'' because she was beautiful, had a large body, and had white hair on her body obeyed Yoshibetsunokami. Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' It is said that Yoshibetsunokami and Hakuhimekami kept horses in the Kozenmine area, and when the horses were sick, they treated them and taught the gods of the country how to raise horses. It is said that Kobirei Shrine was founded when the local people built a shrine and respectfully worshiped the two gods. * Separate story (old story): In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}, a certain princess committed a sin (one theory is {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|lt=Q3541665}}) that she carved an empty ship. It was carried ashore by a boat built by the Japanese government and washed ashore on the coast of Sugaya-go in {{ill|lt=Oshu|Q1175530|WD=Q1175530}}{{ill|lt=Uda District|Q11449246|WD=Q11449246}} (near the present-day Imagami district of Shinchi Town). The princess was pregnant, and with the help of a benevolent man named Gondayu Nukazuka who lived on the coast, he built a wooden hut for her to live in. Afterwards, they traveled along the mountain range and came to the area of ​​Zimei Ridge, where they built a wooden hut and made it their home. The princess gave birth to a child there, but the child's face resembled that of a horse. It is said that Gondayu Nukazuka worked with Hattayu Kanno, who was from the same village, to support the princess and her newborn child ({{ill|lt=Interracial Marriage Story|Q134875778|WD=Q134875778}}). Kobirei Shrine is a shrine that enshrines a child with a face resembling a horse, and Hayama to the south of the shrine is a place where a princess is enshrined, and it is said that it is called ``Mother Mountain'' because her mother is enshrined there. : There is a similar theory in ``Hunai Fudoki'' that a mother, fearing the birth of a child with a horse's face, abandoned her child in a valley, and the {{ill|lt=monkey|Q1367|WD=Q1367|lt=monkey}} who lived in that valley fed the child chewed leaves of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kuzu|UNKNOWN}} tree to raise it, and later enshrined the child as the deity of Kobirei. This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} year ({{ill|lt=702 year|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{ill|lt=Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214|WD=Q108487214}}, who came from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keno clan|UNKNOWN}} clan. Genealogy of the Madarame family name of Marquis Kichiya]” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2016. “Overview of the Keno clan” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2005. . In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," it is listed as ``Shoshimine Shrine,'' and it is listed as a Meishin Taisha Shrine, and it received an official gift from the government (a dedication of a coin on top of the {{ill|lt=Shingu|Q11588759|WD=Q11588759|lt=draft}} from the Jingikan during festivals such as the Tsutsugi Festival and the Niiname Festival).In the Middle Ages, this area belonged to the {{ill|lt=Sendai domain|Q1062182|WD=Q1062182}} and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Date family|UNKNOWN}}, who donated their shrine territory and reconstructed and repaired the shrine, but in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|UNKNOWN}}8 ({{ill|lt=1779|Q7714|WD=Q7714}}), the shrine was destroyed by wildfire. In April of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|WD=Q7818}}), the lord of Sendai rebuilt the shrine, and the shrine was completed in 1806. The shrine hall and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}7 ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), and the shrine building was renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}11 ({{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091}}). Additionally, our company is considered to be the main guardian shrine of the former Uda District. In the old days, it was called ``Katsuzensha'' and ``Souzengu Shrine,'' and it is still called ``Oku no Sozengu Shrine.'' Sozen is a deity also known as ``Aozakigami,'' and is worshiped as the guardian deity of horses in regions ranging from the Tohoku region to the Kanto-Chubu region. === Seven wonders of Kobirei Shrine === There is something called the Seven Wonders within our company's grounds. *Cherry blossoms in the southern sky : It is located on the south side of the company grounds, and is called the ``Nanten cherry tree'' because the flowers bloom in one place like the Nanten fruit. *Hokuto no Matsu : It is located on the east side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and it is said that it curves toward the north because a princess who washed ashore on the shore clung to it during childbirth. *Nana-leaf oak : Located on the west side of the shrine grounds, it was named because its leaves grow in groups of seven. The original ``Nanaha no Kashiwa'' tree fell down in a rainstorm in September 1907, so the chief priest at the time planted an oak sapling in its place. *Kagamigaike : A pond with a circumference of about 3.7 meters located in a valley on the east side of the shrine grounds, with sacred water springing up nearby and flowing into the pond. It is said that this water and pond have never dried up even during any drought. Kagamigaike Pond is named after the legend that a princess used the water in the pond as a mirror. *One-leaf reed (Yoshi) : It is found around Kagamigaike Pond and is said to have leaves only on one side of the stem. It is said that the gods Yoshibetsu and Hakuhime fed horses with this reed as food. *bud white bamboo : This bamboo grows on the hills around Kagamigaike Pond and is called ``Mejiro no Sasa'' because its buds are white. It is said that if a horse eats regular bamboo, it will get sick, but if a horse eats sprouted bamboo, it will not get sick. It is also said that feeding horses with sprouted bamboo soaked in the sacred waters of Kagamigaike is effective against horse illnesses. *Satsuki of Kuyo : It is located on the west side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and is said to have nine flowers blooming in white. Of the Seven Wonders, it is said that the cherry blossoms in the southern sky, the seven-leaved oak, and the nine-leafed oak were brought as plants by a princess when she washed ashore. === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall of Nagarezukuri style. File:Honden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Main shrine
{{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. You can see the Date family crest, the vertical, three-hitter, and nine-day crest. File:Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Precincts of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Precincts There is a valley on the east side of the precincts, and if you go down the path on the slope, you will find a sacred spring and a pond called Kagami-ike Pond. Legend has it that the sacred water never dries up even in times of drought. There is also a shrine office and a parking lot within the precincts, and the hilly area to the south of the precincts is home to Hayama Park, complete with stairs and an observation deck. Inside Hayama Park, there is a place where the enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to have been buried, and a stone monument has been erected that reads, ``The burial place of Kobirei Shrine's enshrined deity.'' File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Sacred water and stone shrine File:Kagami-ike pond of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Kagami-ike pond and bamboo forestFile:Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine. File:View from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|View from Hayama Park to the east
The area around the {{ill|lt=Shinchi Power Plant|Q11501532|WD=Q11501532|lt=Shinchi Thermal Power Plant}} where smoke is rising is the Imakami area where the princess washed ashore. File:South view from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|South view from Hayama-kouen park File:2nd torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG| File:1st torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Setsusha === * Hayama Shrine (Hayama Shrine) :: The enshrined deity is Toyouke Hime no Mikoto, the same as Kobirei Shrine, and it is believed to be a shrine that protects safe childbirth and protects children from illness. Inside the shelter are pillows for praying for safe childbirth and a small shrine. In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.'' File:Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Hayama Shrine File:Inside of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Inside the cover
Enshrines a small shrine and a board sign File:Anzan-ishi(easy delivery stone) of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Easy delivery stone === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Soma District|Q1155850|WD=Q1155850}}{{ill|lt=Shinchi Town|Q1205416|WD=Q1205416}}{{ill|lt=Komagamine (Shinchi Town)|Q11668283|WD=Q11668283|lt=Komagamine}} Daisaku 44 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * “Kobei Shrine Visit Guide” (Kobei Shrine Office) * “Shinchi Town History” (Shinchi Town Board of Education 1993) === External link === * [https://kobiminejinja.jp/ Kobimine Shrine] (official website) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276004.html Kobirei Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kohimine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:新地町の建築物]] [[Category:新地町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名こひみねしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}}''' : {{ill|lt=Toyouke Daijingu|Q11633343|WD=Q11633343|lt=Ise Jingu Outer Shrine}} is said to be the same deity, but there are various theories about the deity enshrined (see below). : Hisao Hoga is the founder of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kodanmine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Komayumi Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} and {{jalink|Komagata Shrine|Q11 in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}. 668370}} (Komagata God) and claimed to be the same god "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm Overview of the Keno clan]” “Koju Kinoboma”, 2005. . It is said that Toyouke Hime no Mikoto has divine virtues such as good harvests, protection of safe childbirth, eradication of pestilence, and protection of sericulture.There is also a legend about the deity enshrined in our company and horses, so there is strong belief in horses, including protection of horses and prayers for recovery from horse illnesses. === About the enshrined deity === The current enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to be Toyouke Hime no Mikoto of the Geku of Ise Jingu, but the following are the main theories regarding the other enshrined deities. * Theory of {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} : In {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}} there was a god called Yoshibetsu no Kami. Yoshibetsu no Kami was a god with a long face, a large and strong body, and a ferocious heart. It is said that for this reason, the gods feared and followed the god of Yoshibetsu. However, many goddesses did not obey Yoshibetsunokami because she had an ugly appearance and a large private part, but only the goddess who was called ``Hakuhime no Kami'' because she was beautiful, had a large body, and had white hair on her body obeyed Yoshibetsunokami. Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' It is said that Yoshibetsunokami and Hakuhimekami kept horses in the Kozenmine area, and when the horses were sick, they treated them and taught the gods of the country how to raise horses. It is said that Kobirei Shrine was founded when the local people built a shrine and respectfully worshiped the two gods. * Separate story (old story): In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}, a certain princess committed a sin (one theory is {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|lt=Q3541665}}) that she carved an empty ship. It was carried ashore by a boat built by the Japanese government and washed ashore on the coast of Sugaya-go in {{ill|lt=Oshu|Q1175530|WD=Q1175530}}{{ill|lt=Uda District|Q11449246|WD=Q11449246}} (near the present-day Imagami district of Shinchi Town). The princess was pregnant, and with the help of a benevolent man named Gondayu Nukazuka who lived on the coast, he built a wooden hut for her to live in. Afterwards, they traveled along the mountain range and came to the area of ​​Zimei Ridge, where they built a wooden hut and made it their home. The princess gave birth to a child there, but the child's face resembled that of a horse. It is said that Gondayu Nukazuka worked with Hattayu Kanno, who was from the same village, to support the princess and her newborn child ({{ill|lt=Interracial Marriage Story|Q134875778|WD=Q134875778}}). Kobirei Shrine is a shrine that enshrines a child with a face resembling a horse, and Hayama to the south of the shrine is a place where a princess is enshrined, and it is said that it is called ``Mother Mountain'' because her mother is enshrined there. : There is a similar theory in ``Hunai Fudoki'' that a mother, fearing the birth of a child with a horse's face, abandoned her child in a valley, and the {{ill|lt=monkey|Q1367|WD=Q1367|lt=monkey}} who lived in that valley fed the child chewed leaves of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kuzu|1=UNKNOWN}} tree to raise it, and later enshrined the child as the deity of Kobirei. This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} year ({{ill|lt=702 year|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{ill|lt=Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214|WD=Q108487214}}, who came from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keno clan|1=UNKNOWN}} clan. Genealogy of the Madarame family name of Marquis Kichiya]” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2016. “Overview of the Keno clan” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2005. . In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," it is listed as ``Shoshimine Shrine,'' and it is listed as a Meishin Taisha Shrine, and it received an official gift from the government (a dedication of a coin on top of the {{ill|lt=Shingu|Q11588759|WD=Q11588759|lt=draft}} from the Jingikan during festivals such as the Tsutsugi Festival and the Niiname Festival).In the Middle Ages, this area belonged to the {{ill|lt=Sendai domain|Q1062182|WD=Q1062182}} and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Date family|1=UNKNOWN}}, who donated their shrine territory and reconstructed and repaired the shrine, but in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}}8 ({{ill|lt=1779|Q7714|WD=Q7714}}), the shrine was destroyed by wildfire. In April of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|WD=Q7818}}), the lord of Sendai rebuilt the shrine, and the shrine was completed in 1806. The shrine hall and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}7 ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), and the shrine building was renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}11 ({{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091}}). Additionally, our company is considered to be the main guardian shrine of the former Uda District. In the old days, it was called ``Katsuzensha'' and ``Souzengu Shrine,'' and it is still called ``Oku no Sozengu Shrine.'' Sozen is a deity also known as ``Aozakigami,'' and is worshiped as the guardian deity of horses in regions ranging from the Tohoku region to the Kanto-Chubu region. === Seven wonders of Kobirei Shrine === There is something called the Seven Wonders within our company's grounds. *Cherry blossoms in the southern sky : It is located on the south side of the company grounds, and is called the ``Nanten cherry tree'' because the flowers bloom in one place like the Nanten fruit. *Hokuto no Matsu : It is located on the east side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and it is said that it curves toward the north because a princess who washed ashore on the shore clung to it during childbirth. *Nana-leaf oak : Located on the west side of the shrine grounds, it was named because its leaves grow in groups of seven. The original ``Nanaha no Kashiwa'' tree fell down in a rainstorm in September 1907, so the chief priest at the time planted an oak sapling in its place. *Kagamigaike : A pond with a circumference of about 3.7 meters located in a valley on the east side of the shrine grounds, with sacred water springing up nearby and flowing into the pond. It is said that this water and pond have never dried up even during any drought. Kagamigaike Pond is named after the legend that a princess used the water in the pond as a mirror. *One-leaf reed (Yoshi) : It is found around Kagamigaike Pond and is said to have leaves only on one side of the stem. It is said that the gods Yoshibetsu and Hakuhime fed horses with this reed as food. *bud white bamboo : This bamboo grows on the hills around Kagamigaike Pond and is called ``Mejiro no Sasa'' because its buds are white. It is said that if a horse eats regular bamboo, it will get sick, but if a horse eats sprouted bamboo, it will not get sick. It is also said that feeding horses with sprouted bamboo soaked in the sacred waters of Kagamigaike is effective against horse illnesses. *Satsuki of Kuyo : It is located on the west side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and is said to have nine flowers blooming in white. Of the Seven Wonders, it is said that the cherry blossoms in the southern sky, the seven-leaved oak, and the nine-leafed oak were brought as plants by a princess when she washed ashore. === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall of Nagarezukuri style. File:Honden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Main shrine
{{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. You can see the Date family crest, the vertical, three-hitter, and nine-day crest. File:Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Precincts of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Precincts There is a valley on the east side of the precincts, and if you go down the path on the slope, you will find a sacred spring and a pond called Kagami-ike Pond. Legend has it that the sacred water never dries up even in times of drought. There is also a shrine office and a parking lot within the precincts, and the hilly area to the south of the precincts is home to Hayama Park, complete with stairs and an observation deck. Inside Hayama Park, there is a place where the enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to have been buried, and a stone monument has been erected that reads, ``The burial place of Kobirei Shrine's enshrined deity.'' File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Sacred water and stone shrine File:Kagami-ike pond of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Kagami-ike pond and bamboo forestFile:Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine. File:View from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|View from Hayama Park to the east
The area around the {{ill|lt=Shinchi Power Plant|Q11501532|WD=Q11501532|lt=Shinchi Thermal Power Plant}} where smoke is rising is the Imakami area where the princess washed ashore. File:South view from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|South view from Hayama-kouen park File:2nd torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG| File:1st torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Setsusha === * Hayama Shrine (Hayama Shrine) :: The enshrined deity is Toyouke Hime no Mikoto, the same as Kobirei Shrine, and it is believed to be a shrine that protects safe childbirth and protects children from illness. Inside the shelter are pillows for praying for safe childbirth and a small shrine. In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.'' File:Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Hayama Shrine File:Inside of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Inside the cover
Enshrines a small shrine and a board sign File:Anzan-ishi(easy delivery stone) of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Easy delivery stone === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Soma District|Q1155850|WD=Q1155850}}{{ill|lt=Shinchi Town|Q1205416|WD=Q1205416}}{{ill|lt=Komagamine (Shinchi Town)|Q11668283|WD=Q11668283|lt=Komagamine}} Daisaku 44 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * “Kobei Shrine Visit Guide” (Kobei Shrine Office) * “Shinchi Town History” (Shinchi Town Board of Education 1993) === External link === * [https://kobiminejinja.jp/ Kobimine Shrine] (official website) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276004.html Kobirei Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kohimine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:新地町の建築物]] [[Category:新地町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名こひみねしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' It is said that Yoshibetsunokami and Hakuhimekami kept horses in the Kozenmine area, and when the horses were sick, they treated them and taught the gods of the country how to raise horses. It is said that Kobirei Shrine was founded when the local people built a shrine and respectfully worshiped the two gods. * Separate story (old story): In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}, a certain princess committed a sin (one theory is {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|lt=Q3541665}}) that she carved an empty ship. It was carried ashore by a boat built by the Japanese government and washed ashore on the coast of Sugaya-go in {{ill|lt=Oshu|Q1175530|WD=Q1175530}}{{ill|lt=Uda District|Q11449246|WD=Q11449246}} (near the present-day Imagami district of Shinchi Town). The princess was pregnant, and with the help of a benevolent man named Gondayu Nukazuka who lived on the coast, he built a wooden hut for her to live in. Afterwards, they traveled along the mountain range and came to the area of ​​Zimei Ridge, where they built a wooden hut and made it their home. The princess gave birth to a child there, but the child's face resembled that of a horse. It is said that Gondayu Nukazuka worked with Hattayu Kanno, who was from the same village, to support the princess and her newborn child ({{ill|lt=Interracial Marriage Story|Q134875778|WD=Q134875778}}). Kobirei Shrine is a shrine that enshrines a child with a face resembling a horse, and Hayama to the south of the shrine is a place where a princess is enshrined, and it is said that it is called ``Mother Mountain'' because her mother is enshrined there. : There is a similar theory in ``Hunai Fudoki'' that a mother, fearing the birth of a child with a horse's face, abandoned her child in a valley, and the {{ill|lt=monkey|Q1367|WD=Q1367|lt=monkey}} who lived in that valley fed the child chewed leaves of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kuzu|1=UNKNOWN}} tree to raise it, and later enshrined the child as the deity of Kobirei. This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} year ({{ill|lt=702 year|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{ill|lt=Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214|WD=Q108487214}}, who came from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keno clan|1=UNKNOWN}} clan. Genealogy of the Madarame family name of Marquis Kichiya]” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2016. “Overview of the Keno clan” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2005. . In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," it is listed as ``Shoshimine Shrine,'' and it is listed as a Meishin Taisha Shrine, and it received an official gift from the government (a dedication of a coin on top of the {{ill|lt=Shingu|Q11588759|WD=Q11588759|lt=draft}} from the Jingikan during festivals such as the Tsutsugi Festival and the Niiname Festival).In the Middle Ages, this area belonged to the {{ill|lt=Sendai domain|Q1062182|WD=Q1062182}} and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Date family|1=UNKNOWN}}, who donated their shrine territory and reconstructed and repaired the shrine, but in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}}8 ({{ill|lt=1779|Q7714|WD=Q7714}}), the shrine was destroyed by wildfire. In April of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|WD=Q7818}}), the lord of Sendai rebuilt the shrine, and the shrine was completed in 1806. The shrine hall and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}7 ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), and the shrine building was renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}11 ({{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091}}). Additionally, our company is considered to be the main guardian shrine of the former Uda District. In the old days, it was called ``Katsuzensha'' and ``Souzengu Shrine,'' and it is still called ``Oku no Sozengu Shrine.'' Sozen is a deity also known as ``Aozakigami,'' and is worshiped as the guardian deity of horses in regions ranging from the Tohoku region to the Kanto-Chubu region. === Seven wonders of Kobirei Shrine === There is something called the Seven Wonders within our company's grounds. *Cherry blossoms in the southern sky : It is located on the south side of the company grounds, and is called the ``Nanten cherry tree'' because the flowers bloom in one place like the Nanten fruit. *Hokuto no Matsu : It is located on the east side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and it is said that it curves toward the north because a princess who washed ashore on the shore clung to it during childbirth. *Nana-leaf oak : Located on the west side of the shrine grounds, it was named because its leaves grow in groups of seven. The original ``Nanaha no Kashiwa'' tree fell down in a rainstorm in September 1907, so the chief priest at the time planted an oak sapling in its place. *Kagamigaike : A pond with a circumference of about 3.7 meters located in a valley on the east side of the shrine grounds, with sacred water springing up nearby and flowing into the pond. It is said that this water and pond have never dried up even during any drought. Kagamigaike Pond is named after the legend that a princess used the water in the pond as a mirror. *One-leaf reed (Yoshi) : It is found around Kagamigaike Pond and is said to have leaves only on one side of the stem. It is said that the gods Yoshibetsu and Hakuhime fed horses with this reed as food. *bud white bamboo : This bamboo grows on the hills around Kagamigaike Pond and is called ``Mejiro no Sasa'' because its buds are white. It is said that if a horse eats regular bamboo, it will get sick, but if a horse eats sprouted bamboo, it will not get sick. It is also said that feeding horses with sprouted bamboo soaked in the sacred waters of Kagamigaike is effective against horse illnesses. *Satsuki of Kuyo : It is located on the west side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and is said to have nine flowers blooming in white. Of the Seven Wonders, it is said that the cherry blossoms in the southern sky, the seven-leaved oak, and the nine-leafed oak were brought as plants by a princess when she washed ashore. === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall of Nagarezukuri style. File:Honden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Main shrine
{{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. You can see the Date family crest, the vertical, three-hitter, and nine-day crest. File:Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Precincts of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Precincts There is a valley on the east side of the precincts, and if you go down the path on the slope, you will find a sacred spring and a pond called Kagami-ike Pond. Legend has it that the sacred water never dries up even in times of drought. There is also a shrine office and a parking lot within the precincts, and the hilly area to the south of the precincts is home to Hayama Park, complete with stairs and an observation deck. Inside Hayama Park, there is a place where the enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to have been buried, and a stone monument has been erected that reads, ``The burial place of Kobirei Shrine's enshrined deity.'' File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Sacred water and stone shrine File:Kagami-ike pond of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Kagami-ike pond and bamboo forestFile:Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine. File:View from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|View from Hayama Park to the east
The area around the {{ill|lt=Shinchi Power Plant|Q11501532|WD=Q11501532|lt=Shinchi Thermal Power Plant}} where smoke is rising is the Imakami area where the princess washed ashore. File:South view from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|South view from Hayama-kouen park File:2nd torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG| File:1st torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Setsusha === * Hayama Shrine (Hayama Shrine) :: The enshrined deity is Toyouke Hime no Mikoto, the same as Kobirei Shrine, and it is believed to be a shrine that protects safe childbirth and protects children from illness. Inside the shelter are pillows for praying for safe childbirth and a small shrine. In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.'' File:Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Hayama Shrine File:Inside of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Inside the cover
Enshrines a small shrine and a board sign File:Anzan-ishi(easy delivery stone) of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Easy delivery stone === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Soma District|Q1155850|WD=Q1155850}}{{ill|lt=Shinchi Town|Q1205416|WD=Q1205416}}{{ill|lt=Komagamine (Shinchi Town)|Q11668283|WD=Q11668283|lt=Komagamine}} Daisaku 44 === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * “Kobei Shrine Visit Guide” (Kobei Shrine Office) * “Shinchi Town History” (Shinchi Town Board of Education 1993) === External link === * [https://kobiminejinja.jp/ Kobimine Shrine] (official website) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276004.html Kobirei Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kohimine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kobimine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:新地町の建築物]] [[Category:新地町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名こひみねしんしや]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is said that Toyouke Hime no Mikoto has divine virtues such as good harvests, protection of safe childbirth, eradication of pestilence, and protection of sericulture.There is also a legend about the deity enshrined in our company and horses, so there is strong belief in horses, including protection of horses and prayers for recovery from horse illnesses. === About the enshrined deity === The current enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to be Toyouke Hime no Mikoto of the Geku of Ise Jingu, but the following are the main theories regarding the other enshrined deities. * Theory of {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} : In {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}} there was a god called Yoshibetsu no Kami. Yoshibetsu no Kami was a god with a long face, a large and strong body, and a ferocious heart. It is said that for this reason, the gods feared and followed the god of Yoshibetsu. However, many goddesses did not obey Yoshibetsunokami because she had an ugly appearance and a large private part, but only the goddess who was called ``Hakuhime no Kami'' because she was beautiful, had a large body, and had white hair on her body obeyed Yoshibetsunokami. Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' Additionally, our company is considered to be the main guardian shrine of the former Uda District. In the old days, it was called ``Katsuzensha'' and ``Souzengu Shrine,'' and it is still called ``Oku no Sozengu Shrine.'' Sozen is a deity also known as ``Aozakigami,'' and is worshiped as the guardian deity of horses in regions ranging from the Tohoku region to the Kanto-Chubu region. === Seven wonders of Kobirei Shrine === There is something called the Seven Wonders within our company's grounds. *Cherry blossoms in the southern sky : It is located on the south side of the company grounds, and is called the ``Nanten cherry tree'' because the flowers bloom in one place like the Nanten fruit. *Hokuto no Matsu : It is located on the east side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and it is said that it curves toward the north because a princess who washed ashore on the shore clung to it during childbirth. *Nana-leaf oak : Located on the west side of the shrine grounds, it was named because its leaves grow in groups of seven. The original ``Nanaha no Kashiwa'' tree fell down in a rainstorm in September 1907, so the chief priest at the time planted an oak sapling in its place. *Kagamigaike : A pond with a circumference of about 3.7 meters located in a valley on the east side of the shrine grounds, with sacred water springing up nearby and flowing into the pond. It is said that this water and pond have never dried up even during any drought. Kagamigaike Pond is named after the legend that a princess used the water in the pond as a mirror. *One-leaf reed (Yoshi) : It is found around Kagamigaike Pond and is said to have leaves only on one side of the stem. It is said that the gods Yoshibetsu and Hakuhime fed horses with this reed as food. *bud white bamboo : This bamboo grows on the hills around Kagamigaike Pond and is called ``Mejiro no Sasa'' because its buds are white. It is said that if a horse eats regular bamboo, it will get sick, but if a horse eats sprouted bamboo, it will not get sick. It is also said that feeding horses with sprouted bamboo soaked in the sacred waters of Kagamigaike is effective against horse illnesses. *Satsuki of Kuyo : It is located on the west side of the stone stairs in front of the shrine, and is said to have nine flowers blooming in white. Of the Seven Wonders, it is said that the cherry blossoms in the southern sky, the seven-leaved oak, and the nine-leafed oak were brought as plants by a princess when she washed ashore. === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall of Nagarezukuri style. File:Honden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Main shrine
{{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. You can see the Date family crest, the vertical, three-hitter, and nine-day crest. File:Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine 3.JPG|Haiden of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Precincts of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Precincts There is a valley on the east side of the precincts, and if you go down the path on the slope, you will find a sacred spring and a pond called Kagami-ike Pond. Legend has it that the sacred water never dries up even in times of drought. There is also a shrine office and a parking lot within the precincts, and the hilly area to the south of the precincts is home to Hayama Park, complete with stairs and an observation deck. Inside Hayama Park, there is a place where the enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to have been buried, and a stone monument has been erected that reads, ``The burial place of Kobirei Shrine's enshrined deity.'' File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine File:Spring water of Kobimine-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Sacred water and stone shrine File:Kagami-ike pond of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Kagami-ike pond and bamboo forestFile:Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Tomb of Goddess of Kobimine-jinja shrine. File:View from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|View from Hayama Park to the east
The area around the {{ill|lt=Shinchi Power Plant|Q11501532|WD=Q11501532|lt=Shinchi Thermal Power Plant}} where smoke is rising is the Imakami area where the princess washed ashore. File:South view from Hayama-kouen park.JPG|South view from Hayama-kouen park File:2nd torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG| File:1st torii gate of Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii gate === Setsusha === * Hayama Shrine (Hayama Shrine) :: The enshrined deity is Toyouke Hime no Mikoto, the same as Kobirei Shrine, and it is believed to be a shrine that protects safe childbirth and protects children from illness. Inside the shelter are pillows for praying for safe childbirth and a small shrine. In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.'' File:Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Hayama Shrine File:Inside of Hayama-jinja shrine in Kobimine-jinja shrine.JPG|Inside the cover

It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === * '''{{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}}''' : {{ill|lt=Toyouke Daijingu|Q11633343|WD=Q11633343|lt=Ise Jingu Outer Shrine}} is said to be the same deity, but there are various theories about the deity enshrined (see below). : Hisao Hoga is the founder of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kodanmine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Komayumi Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} and {{jalink|Komagata Shrine|Q11 in {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}. 668370}} (Komagata God) and claimed to be the same god "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm Overview of the Keno clan]” “Koju Kinoboma”, 2005. . It is said that Toyouke Hime no Mikoto has divine virtues such as good harvests, protection of safe childbirth, eradication of pestilence, and protection of sericulture.There is also a legend about the deity enshrined in our company and horses, so there is strong belief in horses, including protection of horses and prayers for recovery from horse illnesses. === About the enshrined deity === The current enshrined deity of Kobirei Shrine is said to be Toyouke Hime no Mikoto of the Geku of Ise Jingu, but the following are the main theories regarding the other enshrined deities. * Theory of {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} : In {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}} there was a god called Yoshibetsu no Kami. Yoshibetsu no Kami was a god with a long face, a large and strong body, and a ferocious heart. It is said that for this reason, the gods feared and followed the god of Yoshibetsu. However, many goddesses did not obey Yoshibetsunokami because she had an ugly appearance and a large private part, but only the goddess who was called ``Hakuhime no Kami'' because she was beautiful, had a large body, and had white hair on her body obeyed Yoshibetsunokami. Yoshibetsunokami was very happy, married Hakuhimekami as his wife, and lived here. It is said that the place where the couple deities of Ishibetsu no Kami and Hakuhime no Kami set up their shrine was called ``Komae-mine'' and is now called ``Komei-mine.'' It is said that Yoshibetsunokami and Hakuhimekami kept horses in the Kozenmine area, and when the horses were sick, they treated them and taught the gods of the country how to raise horses. It is said that Kobirei Shrine was founded when the local people built a shrine and respectfully worshiped the two gods. * Separate story (old story): In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}}, a certain princess committed a sin (one theory is {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637|lt=Q3541665}}) that she carved an empty ship. It was carried ashore by a boat built by the Japanese government and washed ashore on the coast of Sugaya-go in {{ill|lt=Oshu|Q1175530|WD=Q1175530}}{{ill|lt=Uda District|Q11449246|WD=Q11449246}} (near the present-day Imagami district of Shinchi Town). The princess was pregnant, and with the help of a benevolent man named Gondayu Nukazuka who lived on the coast, he built a wooden hut for her to live in. Afterwards, they traveled along the mountain range and came to the area of ​​Zimei Ridge, where they built a wooden hut and made it their home. The princess gave birth to a child there, but the child's face resembled that of a horse. It is said that Gondayu Nukazuka worked with Hattayu Kanno, who was from the same village, to support the princess and her newborn child ({{ill|lt=Interracial Marriage Story|Q134875778|WD=Q134875778}}). Kobirei Shrine is a shrine that enshrines a child with a face resembling a horse, and Hayama to the south of the shrine is a place where a princess is enshrined, and it is said that it is called ``Mother Mountain'' because her mother is enshrined there. : There is a similar theory in ``Hunai Fudoki'' that a mother, fearing the birth of a child with a horse's face, abandoned her child in a valley, and the {{ill|lt=monkey|Q1367|WD=Q1367|lt=monkey}} who lived in that valley fed the child chewed leaves of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kuzu|1=UNKNOWN}} tree to raise it, and later enshrined the child as the deity of Kobirei. This theory is said to be based on the local legend that ``monkeys protect the breeding of cows and horses.'' === History === According to the history book, a temporary shrine was established in the second year of the {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} year ({{ill|lt=702 year|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), where it was worshiped for 200 years, and a shrine building was built in the {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} year (907). Its origin is assumed to be by {{ill|lt=Kunizo Ukita|Q108487214|WD=Q108487214}}, who came from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keno clan|1=UNKNOWN}} clan. Genealogy of the Madarame family name of Marquis Kichiya]” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2016. “Overview of the Keno clan” “Koju Ki no Fusama”, 2005. . In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}," it is listed as ``Shoshimine Shrine,'' and it is listed as a Meishin Taisha Shrine, and it received an official gift from the government (a dedication of a coin on top of the {{ill|lt=Shingu|Q11588759|WD=Q11588759|lt=draft}} from the Jingikan during festivals such as the Tsutsugi Festival and the Niiname Festival).In the Middle Ages, this area belonged to the {{ill|lt=Sendai domain|Q1062182|WD=Q1062182}} and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Date family|1=UNKNOWN}}, who donated their shrine territory and reconstructed and repaired the shrine, but in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=An'ei|1=UNKNOWN}}8 ({{ill|lt=1779|Q7714|WD=Q7714}}), the shrine was destroyed by wildfire. In April of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|WD=Q7818}}), the lord of Sendai rebuilt the shrine, and the shrine was completed in 1806. The shrine hall and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}7 ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|WD=Q2094}}), and the shrine building was renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}11 ({{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091}}). In addition, a red stone called ``Asanseki'' is enshrined in front of the shrine, and it is said that the reason this stone is red is because ``when the enshrined deity gave birth to a child, it was covered in blood.''
